  "text": "Both Kubernetes (v1.32.0) and Grafana (v11.4.0) use the Prometheus client_golang library to implement metrics exposition, but they organize their metrics infrastructure quite differently.\n\n## Kubernetes (kubernetes/kubernetes)\n\n### Core Metrics Infrastructure: k8s.io/component-base/metrics\n\nKubernetes builds a **stability-framework wrapper** around the raw Prometheus library, located in `staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/`.\n\n**Registry layer** (`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/registry.go`):\n- `KubeRegistry` interface wraps `prometheus.Registerer`/`prometheus.Gatherer` with Kubernetes-specific lifecycle methods (`Register`, `MustRegister`, `CustomRegister`, `CustomMustRegister`, `RawMustRegister`, `Gather`, `Reset`, `RegisterMetaMetrics`).\n- `kubeRegistry` struct implements `KubeRegistry`, holding a `PromRegistry` (a `prometheus.Registry`), the binary version (semver), and maps of `hiddenCollectors` and `stableCollectors`.\n- `NewKubeRegistry()` / `newKubeRegistry()` construct a new instance, appending it to a global `registries` slice so that `SetShowHidden()` can re-register hidden metrics at runtime.\n- The `Registerable` interface requires `prometheus.Collector` + `Create(version)`, `ClearState()`, and `FQName()` — enabling version-gated metric hiding/deprecation.\n- `SetShowHidden()`, `SetDisabledMetric()`, `ValidateShowHiddenMetricsVersion()` implement Kubernetes's metric deprecation lifecycle.\n- Meta-metrics `registeredMetricsTotal`, `disabledMetricsTotal`, `hiddenMetricsTotal` are registered via `RegisterMetaMetrics()`.\n\n**Global/legacy registry** (`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/legacyregistry/registry.go`):\n- `defaultRegistry` is the singleton `KubeRegistry` used by nearly all Kubernetes components.\n- Package-level `MustRegister`, `Register`, `RawMustRegister`, `CustomRegister`, `CustomMustRegister`, `Registerer`, `DefaultGatherer` functions delegate to `defaultRegistry`.\n- In `init()`, process and Go runtime collectors (`collectors.NewProcessCollector`, `collectors.NewGoCollector`) are registered, and meta-metrics are initialized.\n- `Handler()` returns an HTTP handler via `promhttp.InstrumentMetricHandler(prometheus.DefaultRegisterer, promhttp.HandlerFor(defaultRegistry, ...))` — this is the core exposition handler.\n- `HandlerWithReset()` additionally supports DELETE to reset all metric values.\n\n**HTTP exposition** (`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/http.go`):\n- `HandlerFor(reg Gatherer, opts HandlerOpts)` wraps `promhttp.HandlerFor` with Kubernetes-specific opts (process start time).\n- `HandlerWithReset(reg KubeRegistry, opts HandlerOpts)` wraps the handler to call `reg.Reset()` on DELETE requests.\n\n**Collector abstraction** (`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/collector.go`):\n- `StableCollector` interface extends `prometheus.Collector` with `DescribeWithStability`, `CollectWithStability`, `Create(version, self)`, `ClearState()`, `HiddenMetrics()`.\n- `BaseStableCollector` provides a base implementation supporting hidden/registerable descriptor tracking per binary version.\n\n**Metric type wrappers**: `counter.go` (`Counter`), `gauge.go` (`Gauge`), `histogram.go` (`Histogram`), `summary.go` (`Summary`) all wrap their Prometheus counterparts with a `lazyMetric` pattern (lazy initialization gated on `Create(version)`).\n\n**Prometheus exposition endpoint installation**:\n- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes/metrics.go`: `DefaultMetrics` and `MetricsWithReset` types install the `/metrics` endpoint on a `mux.PathRecorderMux` via `c.Handle(\"/metrics\", legacyregistry.Handler())`. The `register()` function triggers registration of apiserver-specific metric collectors (`apimetrics.Register()`, `cachermetrics.Register()`, `etcd3metrics.Register()`, `flowcontrolmetrics.Register()`, `peerproxymetrics.Register()`, `handlersmetrics.Register()`).\n- `staging/src/k8s.io/controller-manager/app/serve.go`: `NewBaseHandler()` adds `mux.Handle(\"/metrics\", legacyregistry.Handler())` for controller-manager components.\n- `cmd/kube-proxy/app/server.go` and `cmd/kube-scheduler/app/server.go` similarly call `legacyregistry.Handler()` to install the `/metrics` endpoint.\n- `pkg/kubelet/server/server.go` wires the kubelet's `/metrics` endpoint.\n\n**Sub-packages for domain-specific metrics collectors** (`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/prometheus/`):\n- `workqueue/metrics.go` — registers workqueue metrics\n- `feature/metrics.go` — feature gate metrics\n- `slis/metrics.go`, `slis/registry.go`, `slis/routes.go` — SLI/SLO metrics with dedicated HTTP routes\n- `restclient/metrics.go` — REST client metrics\n- `clientgo/metrics.go` — client-go metrics aggregator (imports leaderelection, restclient, workqueue)\n- `controllers/metrics.go`, `meta/metrics.go`, `version/metrics.go` — controller and version metrics\n\n---\n\n## Grafana (grafana/grafana)\n\nGrafana uses the Prometheus client_golang library more directly, with its own registry provisioning and a dual-gatherer model that combines Kubernetes-style (via `k8s.io/component-base/metrics/legacyregistry`) and native Prometheus registries.\n\n**Core metrics package** (`pkg/infra/metrics/`):\n\n- `metrics.go`: Declares `ExporterName = \"grafana\"` as the Prometheus namespace prefix and defines all global metric variables as bare `prometheus.*` types (e.g., `MInstanceStart prometheus.Counter`, `MPageStatus *prometheus.CounterVec`, `MStatTotalDashboards prometheus.Gauge`, etc.).\n  - `init()` sets up metric objects with the `grafana` namespace prefix.\n  - `initMetricVars(reg prometheus.Registerer)` performs bulk registration of all core Grafana metrics via `reg.MustRegister(...)`.\n\n- `service.go`: `ProvideService(cfg, reg prometheus.Registerer)` is the wire injection point that calls `initMetricVars(reg)` and `initFrontendMetrics(reg)` to register all metrics.\n  - `InternalMetricsService` is a background service that also bridges metrics to Graphite via `graphitebridge.NewBridge`.\n  - `ProvideRegisterer()` returns `legacyregistry.Registerer()` (from Kubernetes's component-base) as the global registerer — meaning Grafana reuses the Kubernetes global registry for metric registration.\n  - `ProvideGatherer()` constructs a **composite gatherer**: `newMultiRegistry(newAddPrefixWrapper(legacyregistry.DefaultGatherer), prometheus.DefaultGatherer)`. This combines metrics from the Kubernetes-style registry (with a `grafana_` prefix enforcement) and the default Prometheus registry.\n  - `addPrefixWrapper` ensures all metric names are prefixed with `grafana_` unless they already start with `grafana_` or `go_`.\n  - `multiRegistry` deduplicates metric names across the two gatherers (allowing `go_*` duplicates).\n\n- `frontendmetrics.go`: `initFrontendMetrics(r prometheus.Registerer)` registers frontend-specific histograms (boot time, first paint, etc.) under the `grafana` namespace.\n\n- `settings.go`: `readSettings()` configures the Graphite bridge interval and address; `parseGraphiteSettings()` populates a `graphitebridge.Config` using `prometheus.DefaultGatherer` for the bridge source.\n\n- `metricutil/utils.go`: Helper functions `NewCounterStartingAtZero` and `NewCounterVecStartingAtZero` initialize counters with a zero observation to guarantee metric time-series existence from startup.\n\n**HTTP /metrics endpoint exposition**:\n- `pkg/server/instrumentation_service.go`: `instrumentationService` / `NewInstrumentationService` creates a standalone HTTP server with `newInstrumentationServer()`, which registers `promhttp.Handler()` (using Prometheus's default gatherer) on `/metrics`. This provides an instrumentation-only endpoint on a separate port.\n- `pkg/api/http_server.go`: The main Grafana HTTP server (`HTTPServer`) also serves `/metrics` by calling `promhttp.HandlerFor(hs.promGatherer, promhttp.HandlerOpts{EnableOpenMetrics: true})` — using the composite `promGatherer` (which combines the Kubernetes legacy registry with the Prometheus default registry). This endpoint supports OpenMetrics format and optional basic authentication.\n\n**Request-level metrics middleware** (`pkg/middleware/request_metrics.go`): `RequestMetrics` is a web middleware that instruments HTTP requests with `http_request_in_flight` gauge and `http_request_duration_seconds` histogram, registered directly against the injected `prometheus.Registerer`.\n\n**Subsystem-specific metric collectors** (each subsystem creates its own scoped registry/sub-namespace):\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/metrics/` — alerting subsystem (scheduler, alertmanager, API, historian, state, multi-org, remote alertmanager, remote writer)\n- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/metrics.go`, `pkg/services/authn/authnimpl/metrics.go`, `pkg/services/auth/idimpl/metrics.go` — auth subsystem metrics\n- `pkg/services/dashboards/service/metrics.go`, `pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/metrics.go` — storage metrics\n- `pkg/infra/httpclient/httpclientprovider/prometheus_metrics_middleware.go` — HTTP client metrics\n\n---\n\n## Comparison\n\n| Aspect | Kubernetes | Grafana |\n|---|---|---|  \n| **Registry** | Custom `KubeRegistry` wrapping `prometheus.Registry`; global singleton in `legacyregistry.defaultRegistry` | Injects `prometheus.Registerer` (backed by k8s `legacyregistry.Registerer()`); composite gatherer combining two registries |\n| **Collector abstraction** | `Registerable` + `StableCollector` / `BaseStableCollector` for version-gated metric hiding | Direct `prometheus.Collector` types; no versioned lifecycle |\n| **Metric types** | Custom wrappers (`Counter`, `Gauge`, `Histogram`, `Summary`) with lazy initialization and stability levels | Native `prometheus.*` types directly |\n| **/metrics endpoint** | `legacyregistry.Handler()` → `promhttp.HandlerFor(defaultRegistry, ...)` installed via `DefaultMetrics.Install()` or `mux.Handle()` in each component | `promhttp.HandlerFor(compositeGatherer, ...)` in `HTTPServer`; separate `instrumentationService` with `promhttp.Handler()` |\n| **OpenMetrics** | Not explicitly enabled | Enabled via `HandlerOpts{EnableOpenMetrics: true}` |\n| **Extra exposition** | DELETE method on `/metrics` resets counters (`HandlerWithReset`) | Optional basic auth on `/metrics` endpoint |\n| **Metric naming** | Namespaced by subsystem (no global prefix enforcement) | `grafana_` namespace prefix enforced by `addPrefixWrapper` on the k8s legacy gatherer |\n| **Side-channel export** | Not built-in | Graphite bridge (`graphitebridge`) for pushing metrics |\n| **Subsystem organization** | Many per-package `metrics.go` files, each calling `legacyregistry.MustRegister` in a `Register()` function or `init()` | Per-subsystem `metrics.go` files that take `prometheus.Registerer` as a constructor argument |"
}

# Prometheus Metrics Exposition Pattern Across Kubernetes and Grafana

## Your Task

Find the Go source files in both kubernetes/kubernetes and grafana/grafana that implement the Prometheus metrics exposition pattern: where metrics are registered using the Prometheus client_golang library, how metric collectors are organized, and where the `/metrics` HTTP endpoint is configured to expose them. Compare how both projects structure their metrics infrastructure.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the crossorg domain. Both Kubernetes and Grafana use the Prometheus client_golang library to expose internal metrics, but they organize their metrics registration and exposition differently. Your goal is to map these patterns across both codebases.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0, sg-evals/grafana--v11.4.0.


## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [],
```

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Kubernetes Metrics Exposition Pattern

**Core infrastructure** in `staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/`:
- `registry.go`: `KubeRegistry` interface + `kubeRegistry` struct wrap `prometheus.Registry` with a version-gated stability framework (`Registerable`, `StableCollector`). The global singleton `defaultRegistry` lives in `legacyregistry/registry.go`.
- `legacyregistry/registry.go`: Package-level `MustRegister`, `Register`, `Handler()`, `HandlerWithReset()` functions delegate to `defaultRegistry`. `Handler()` wraps `promhttp.HandlerFor(defaultRegistry, ...)`.
- `http.go`: `HandlerFor` / `HandlerWithReset` wrap `promhttp` handlers.
- `collector.go`: `StableCollector` / `BaseStableCollector` enable version-gated hiding of metrics.
- **`/metrics` endpoint**: Installed by `DefaultMetrics.Install()` or `MetricsWithReset.Install()` in `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes/metrics.go`, calling `legacyregistry.Handler()`. Also wired in `controller-manager/app/serve.go`, `cmd/kube-proxy`, `cmd/kube-scheduler`, `pkg/kubelet/server/server.go`.

## Grafana Metrics Exposition Pattern

**Core infrastructure** in `pkg/infra/metrics/`:
- `metrics.go`: Global metric variables (counters, gauges, histograms) declared as bare `prometheus.*` types, registered in bulk by `initMetricVars(reg)`.
- `service.go`: `ProvideRegisterer()` returns the Kubernetes `legacyregistry.Registerer()`; `ProvideGatherer()` returns a **composite gatherer** combining `addPrefixWrapper(legacyregistry.DefaultGatherer)` + `prometheus.DefaultGatherer`. The `addPrefixWrapper` enforces `grafana_` prefix on all metric names.
- **`/metrics` endpoint**: Served two ways — `pkg/server/instrumentation_service.go` (`instrumentationService`) runs a dedicated HTTP server using `promhttp.Handler()`, while `pkg/api/http_server.go` serves the same path via `promhttp.HandlerFor(compositeGatherer, HandlerOpts{EnableOpenMetrics: true})` with optional basic auth.

    16→
    17→package legacyregistry
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"net/http"
    21→	"time"
    22→
    23→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"
    24→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/collectors"
    25→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promhttp"
    26→
    27→	"k8s.io/component-base/metrics"
    28→)
    29→
    30→var (
    31→	defaultRegistry = metrics.NewKubeRegistry()
    32→	// DefaultGatherer exposes the global registry gatherer
    33→	DefaultGatherer metrics.Gatherer = defaultRegistry
    34→	// Reset calls reset on the global registry
    35→	Reset = defaultRegistry.Reset
    36→	// MustRegister registers registerable metrics but uses the global registry.
    37→	MustRegister = defaultRegistry.MustRegister
    38→	// RawMustRegister registers prometheus collectors but uses the global registry, this
    39→	// bypasses the metric stability framework
    40→	//
    41→	// Deprecated
    42→	RawMustRegister = defaultRegistry.RawMustRegister
    43→
    44→	// Register registers a collectable metric but uses the global registry
    45→	Register = defaultRegistry.Register
    46→
    47→	// Registerer exposes the global registerer
    48→	Registerer = defaultRegistry.Registerer
    49→
    50→	processStart time.Time
    51→)
    52→
    53→func init() {
    54→	RawMustRegister(collectors.NewProcessCollector(collectors.ProcessCollectorOpts{}))
    55→	RawMustRegister(collectors.NewGoCollector(collectors.WithGoCollectorRuntimeMetrics(collectors.MetricsAll)))
    56→	defaultRegistry.RegisterMetaMetrics()
    57→	processStart = time.Now()
    58→}
    59→
    60→// Handler returns an HTTP handler for the DefaultGatherer. It is
    61→// already instrumented with InstrumentHandler (using "prometheus" as handler
    62→// name).
    63→func Handler() http.Handler {
    64→	return promhttp.InstrumentMetricHandler(prometheus.DefaultRegisterer, promhttp.HandlerFor(defaultRegistry, promhttp.HandlerOpts{ProcessStartTime: processStart}))
    65→}
    66→
    67→// HandlerWithReset returns an HTTP handler for the DefaultGatherer but invokes
    68→// registry reset if the http method is DELETE.
    69→func HandlerWithReset() http.Handler {
    70→	return promhttp.InstrumentMetricHandler(
    71→		prometheus.DefaultRegisterer,
    72→		metrics.HandlerWithReset(defaultRegistry, metrics.HandlerOpts{ProcessStartTime: processStart}))
    73→}
    74→
    75→// CustomRegister registers a custom collector but uses the global registry.
    76→func CustomRegister(c metrics.StableCollector) error {
    77→	err := defaultRegistry.CustomRegister(c)
    78→
    79→	//TODO(RainbowMango): Maybe we can wrap this error by error wrapping.(Golang 1.13)
    80→	_ = prometheus.Register(c)
    81→
    82→	return err
    83→}
    84→
    85→// CustomMustRegister registers custom collectors but uses the global registry.
    86→func CustomMustRegister(cs ...metrics.StableCollector) {
    87→	defaultRegistry.CustomMustRegister(cs...)
    88→
    89→	for _, c := range cs {
    90→		prometheus.MustRegister(c)
    91→	}
    92→}
    93→

(\n\tdefaultRegistry = metrics.NewKubeRegistry()\n\t// DefaultGatherer exposes the global registry gatherer\n\tDefaultGatherer metrics.Gatherer = defaultRegistry\n\t// Reset calls reset on the global registry\n\tReset = defaultRegistry.Reset\n\t// MustRegister registers registerable metrics but uses the global registry.\n\tMustRegister = defaultRegistry.MustRegister\n\t// RawMustRegister registers prometheus collectors but uses the global registry, this\n\t// bypasses the metric stability framework\n\t//\n\t// Deprecated\n\tRawMustRegister = defaultRegistry.RawMustRegister\n\n\t// Register registers a collectable metric but uses the global registry\n\tRegister = defaultRegistry.Register\n\n\t// Registerer exposes the global registerer\n\tRegisterer = defaultRegistry.Registerer\n\n\tprocessStart time.Time\n)\n\nfunc init() {\n\tRawMustRegister(collectors.NewProcessCollector(collectors.ProcessCollectorOpts{}))\n\tRawMustRegister(collectors.NewGoCollector(collectors.WithGoCollectorRuntimeMetrics(collectors.MetricsAll)))\n\tdefaultRegistry.RegisterMetaMetrics()\n\tprocessStart     17→package metrics
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"fmt"
    21→	"sync"
    22→	"sync/atomic"
    23→
    24→	"github.com/blang/semver/v4"
    25→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"
    26→	dto "github.com/prometheus/client_model/go"
    27→
    28→	apimachineryversion "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/version"
    29→	"k8s.io/component-base/version"
    30→)
    31→
    32→var (
    33→	showHiddenOnce      sync.Once
    34→	disabledMetricsLock sync.RWMutex
    35→	showHidden          atomic.Bool
    36→	registries          []*kubeRegistry // stores all registries created by NewKubeRegistry()
    37→	registriesLock      sync.RWMutex
    38→	disabledMetrics     = map[string]struct{}{}
    39→
    40→	registeredMetricsTotal = NewCounterVec(
    41→		&CounterOpts{
    42→			Name:           "registered_metrics_total",
    43→			Help:           "The count of registered metrics broken by stability level and deprecation version.",
    44→			StabilityLevel: BETA,
    45→		},
    46→		[]string{"stability_level", "deprecated_version"},
    47→	)
    48→
    49→	disabledMetricsTotal = NewCounter(
    50→		&CounterOpts{
    51→			Name:           "disabled_metrics_total",
    52→			Help:           "The count of disabled metrics.",
    53→			StabilityLevel: BETA,
    54→		},
    55→	)
    56→
    57→	hiddenMetricsTotal = NewCounter(
    58→		&CounterOpts{
    59→			Name:           "hidden_metrics_total",
    60→			Help:           "The count of hidden metrics.",
    61→			StabilityLevel: BETA,
    62→		},
    63→	)
    64→
    65→	cardinalityEnforcementUnexpectedCategorizationsTotal = NewCounter(
    66→		&CounterOpts{
    67→			Name:           "cardinality_enforcement_unexpected_categorizations_total",
    68→			Help:           "The count of unexpected categorizations during cardinality enforcement.",
    69→			StabilityLevel: ALPHA,
    70→		},
    71→	)
    72→)
    73→
    74→// shouldHide be used to check if a specific metric with deprecated version should be hidden
    75→// according to metrics deprecation lifecycle.
    76→func shouldHide(currentVersion *semver.Version, deprecatedVersion *semver.Version) bool {
    77→	guardVersion, err := semver.Make(fmt.Sprintf("%d.%d.0", currentVersion.Major, currentVersion.Minor))
    78→	if err != nil {
    79→		panic("failed to make version from current version")
    80→	}
    81→
    82→	if deprecatedVersion.LT(guardVersion) {
    83→		return true
    84→	}
    85→
    86→	return false
    87→}
    88→
    89→// ValidateShowHiddenMetricsVersion checks invalid version for which show hidden metrics.
    90→func ValidateShowHiddenMetricsVersion(v string) []error {
    91→	err := validateShowHiddenMetricsVersion(parseVersion(version.Get()), v)
    92→	if err != nil {
    93→		return []error{err}
    94→	}
    95→
    96→	return nil
    97→}
    98→
    99→func SetDisabledMetric(name string) {
   100→	disabledMetricsLock.Lock()
   101→	defer disabledMetricsLock.Unlock()
   102→	disabledMetrics[name] = struct{}{}
   103→	disabledMetricsTotal.Inc()
   104→}
   105→
   106→// SetShowHidden will enable showing hidden metrics. This will no-opt
   107→// after the initial call
   108→func SetShowHidden() {
   109→	showHiddenOnce.Do(func() {
   110→		showHidden.Store(true)
   111→
   112→		// re-register collectors that has been hidden in phase of last registry.
   113→		for _, r := range registries {
   114→			r.enableHiddenCollectors()
   115→			r.enableHiddenStableCollectors()
   116→		}
   117→	})
   118→}
   119→
   120→// ShouldShowHidden returns whether showing hidden deprecated metrics
   121→// is enabled. While the primary usecase for this is internal (to determine
   122→// registration behavior) this can also be used to introspect
   123→func ShouldShowHidden() bool {
   124→	return showHidden.Load()
   125→}
   126→
   127→// Registerable is an interface for a collector metric which we
   128→// will register with KubeRegistry.
   129→type Registerable interface {
   130→	prometheus.Collector
   131→
   132→	// Create will mark deprecated state for the collector
   133→	Create(version *semver.Version) bool
   134→
   135→	// ClearState will clear all the states marked by Create.
   136→	ClearState()
   137→
   138→	// FQName returns the fully-qualified metric name of the collector.
   139→	FQName() string
   140→}
   141→
   142→type resettable interface {
   143→	Reset()
   144→}
   145→
   146→// KubeRegistry is an interface which implements a subset of prometheus.Registerer and
   147→// prometheus.Gatherer interfaces
   148→type KubeRegistry interface {
   149→	// Deprecated
   150→	RawMustRegister(...prometheus.Collector)
   151→	// CustomRegister is our internal variant of Prometheus registry.Register
   152→	CustomRegister(c StableCollector) error
   153→	// CustomMustRegister is our internal variant of Prometheus registry.MustRegister
   154→	CustomMustRegister(cs ...StableCollector)
   155→	// Register conforms to Prometheus registry.Register
   156→	Register(Registerable) error
   157→	// MustRegister conforms to Prometheus registry.MustRegister
   158→	MustRegister(...Registerable)
   159→	// Unregister conforms to Prometheus registry.Unregister
   160→	Unregister(collector Collector) bool
   161→	// Gather conforms to Prometheus gatherer.Gather
   162→	Gather() ([]*dto.MetricFamily, error)
   163→	// Reset invokes the Reset() function on all items in the registry
   164→	// which are added as resettables.
   165→	Reset()
   166→	// RegisterMetaMetrics registers metrics about the number of registered metrics.
   167→	RegisterMetaMetrics()
   168→	// Registerer exposes the underlying prometheus registerer
   169→	Registerer() prometheus.Registerer
   170→	// Gatherer exposes the underlying prometheus gatherer
   171→	Gatherer() prometheus.Gatherer
   172→}
   173→
   174→// kubeRegistry is a wrapper around a prometheus registry-type object. Upon initialization
   175→// the kubernetes binary version information is loaded into the registry object, so that
   176→// automatic behavior can be configured for metric versioning.
   177→type kubeRegistry struct {
   178→	PromRegistry
   179→	version              semver.Version
   180→	hiddenCollectors     map[string]Registerable // stores all collectors that has been hidden
   181→	stableCollectors     []StableCollector       // stores all stable collector
   182→	hiddenCollectorsLock sync.RWMutex
   183→	stableCollectorsLock sync.RWMutex
   184→	resetLock            sync.RWMutex
   185→	resettables          []resettable
   186→}
   187→
   188→// Register registers a new Collector to be included in metrics
   189→// collection. It returns an error if the descriptors provided by the
   190→// Collector are invalid or if they — in combination with descriptors of
   191→// already registered Collectors — do not fulfill the consistency and
   192→// uniqueness criteria described in the documentation of metric.Desc.
   193→func (kr *kubeRegistry) Register(c Registerable) error {
   194→	if c.Create(&kr.version) {
   195→		defer kr.addResettable(c)
   196→		return kr.PromRegistry.Register(c)
   197→	}
   198→
   199→	kr.trackHiddenCollector(c)
   200→	return nil
   201→}
   202→
   203→// Registerer exposes the underlying prometheus.Registerer
   204→func (kr *kubeRegistry) Registerer() prometheus.Registerer {
   205→	return kr.PromRegistry
   206→}
   207→
   208→// Gatherer exposes the underlying prometheus.Gatherer
   209→func (kr *kubeRegistry) Gatherer() prometheus.Gatherer {
   210→	return kr.PromRegistry
   211→}
   212→
   213→// MustRegister works like Register but registers any number of
   214→// Collectors and panics upon the first registration that causes an
   215→// error.
   216→func (kr *kubeRegistry) MustRegister(cs ...Registerable) {
   217→	metrics := make([]prometheus.Collector, 0, len(cs))
   218→	for _, c := range cs {
   219→		if c.Create(&kr.version) {
   220→			metrics = append(metrics, c)
   221→			kr.addResettable(c)
   222→		} else {
   223→			kr.trackHiddenCollector(c)
   224→		}
   225→	}
   226→	kr.PromRegistry.MustRegister(metrics...)
   227→}
   228→
   229→// CustomRegister registers a new custom collector.
   230→func (kr *kubeRegistry) CustomRegister(c StableCollector) error {
   231→	kr.trackStableCollectors(c)
   232→	defer kr.addResettable(c)
   233→	if c.Create(&kr.version, c) {
   234→		return kr.PromRegistry.Register(c)
   235→	}
   236→	return nil
   237→}
   238→
   239→// CustomMustRegister works like CustomRegister but registers any number of
   240→// StableCollectors and panics upon the first registration that causes an
   241→// error.
   242→func (kr *kubeRegistry) CustomMustRegister(cs ...StableCollector) {
   243→	kr.trackStableCollectors(cs...)
   244→	collectors := make([]prometheus.Collector, 0, len(cs))
   245→	for _, c := range cs {
   246→		if c.Create(&kr.version, c) {
   247→			kr.addResettable(c)
   248→			collectors = append(collectors, c)
   249→		}
   250→	}
   251→	kr.PromRegistry.MustRegister(collectors...)
   252→}
   253→
   254→// RawMustRegister takes a native prometheus.Collector and registers the collector
   255→// to the registry. This bypasses metrics safety checks, so should only be used
   256→// to register custom prometheus collectors.
   257→//
   258→// Deprecated
   259→func (kr *kubeRegistry) RawMustRegister(cs ...prometheus.Collector) {
   260→	kr.PromRegistry.MustRegister(cs...)
   261→	for _, c := range cs {
   262→		kr.addResettable(c)
   263→	}
   264→}
   265→
   266→// addResettable will automatically add our metric to our reset
   267→// list if it satisfies the interface
   268→func (kr *kubeRegistry) addResettable(i interface{}) {
   269→	kr.resetLock.Lock()
   270→	defer kr.resetLock.Unlock()
   271→	if resettable, ok := i.(resettable); ok {
   272→		kr.resettables = append(kr.resettables, resettable)
   273→	}
   274→}
   275→
   276→// Unregister unregisters the Collector that equals the Collector passed
   277→// in as an argument.  (Two Collectors are considered equal if their
   278→// Describe method yields the same set of descriptors.) The function
   279→// returns whether a Collector was unregistered. Note that an unchecked
   280→// Collector cannot be unregistered (as its Describe method does not
   281→// yield any descriptor).
   282→func (kr *kubeRegistry) Unregister(collector Collector) bool {
   283→	return kr.PromRegistry.Unregister(collector)
   284→}
   285→
   286→// Gather calls the Collect method of the registered Collectors and then
   287→// gathers the collected metrics into a lexicographically sorted slice
   288→// of uniquely named MetricFamily protobufs. Gather ensures that the
   289→// returned slice is valid and self-consistent so that it can be used
   290→// for valid exposition. As an exception to the strict consistency
   291→// requirements described for metric.Desc, Gather will tolerate
   292→// different sets of label names for metrics of the same metric family.
   293→func (kr *kubeRegistry) Gather() ([]*dto.MetricFamily, error) {
   294→	return kr.PromRegistry.Gather()
   295→}
   296→
   297→// trackHiddenCollector stores all hidden collectors.
   298→func (kr *kubeRegistry) trackHiddenCollector(c Registerable) {
   299→	kr.hiddenCollectorsLock.Lock()
   300→	defer kr.hiddenCollectorsLock.Unlock()
   301→
   302→	kr.hiddenCollectors[c.FQName()] = c
   303→	hiddenMetricsTotal.Inc()
   304→}
   305→
   306→// trackStableCollectors stores all custom collectors.
   307→func (kr *kubeRegistry) trackStableCollectors(cs ...StableCollector) {
   308→	kr.stableCollectorsLock.Lock()
   309→	defer kr.stableCollectorsLock.Unlock()
   310→
   311→	kr.stableCollectors = append(kr.stableCollectors, cs...)
   312→}
   313→
   314→// enableHiddenCollectors will re-register all of the hidden collectors.
   315→func (kr *kubeRegistry) enableHiddenCollectors() {
   316→	if len(kr.hiddenCollectors) == 0 {
   317→		return
   318→	}
   319→
   320→	kr.hiddenCollectorsLock.Lock()
   321→	cs := make([]Registerable, 0, len(kr.hiddenCollectors))
   322→
   323→	for _, c := range kr.hiddenCollectors {
   324→		c.ClearState()
   325→		cs = append(cs, c)
   326→	}
   327→
   328→	kr.hiddenCollectors = make(map[string]Registerable)
   329→	kr.hiddenCollectorsLock.Unlock()
   330→	kr.MustRegister(cs...)
   331→}
   332→
   333→// enableHiddenStableCollectors will re-register the stable collectors if there is one or more hidden metrics in it.
   334→// Since we can not register a metrics twice, so we have to unregister first then register again.
   335→func (kr *kubeRegistry) enableHiddenStableCollectors() {
   336→	if len(kr.stableCollectors) == 0 {
   337→		return
   338→	}
   339→
   340→	kr.stableCollectorsLock.Lock()
   341→
   342→	cs := make([]StableCollector, 0, len(kr.stableCollectors))
   343→	for _, c := range kr.stableCollectors {
   344→		if len(c.HiddenMetrics()) > 0 {
   345→			kr.Unregister(c) // unregister must happens before clear state, otherwise no metrics would be unregister
   346→			c.ClearState()
   347→			cs = append(cs, c)
   348→		}
   349→	}
   350→
   351→	kr.stableCollectors = nil
   352→	kr.stableCollectorsLock.Unlock()
   353→	kr.CustomMustRegister(cs...)
   354→}
   355→
   356→// Reset invokes Reset on all metrics that are resettable.
   357→func (kr *kubeRegistry) Reset() {
   358→	kr.resetLock.RLock()
   359→	defer kr.resetLock.RUnlock()
   360→	for _, r := range kr.resettables {
   361→		r.Reset()
   362→	}
   363→}
   364→
   365→// BuildVersion is a helper function that can be easily mocked.
   366→var BuildVersion = version.Get
   367→
   368→func newKubeRegistry(v apimachineryversion.Info) *kubeRegistry {
   369→	r := &kubeRegistry{
   370→		PromRegistry:     prometheus.NewRegistry(),
   371→		version:          parseVersion(v),
   372→		hiddenCollectors: make(map[string]Registerable),
   373→		resettables:      make([]resettable, 0),
   374→	}
   375→
   376→	registriesLock.Lock()
   377→	defer registriesLock.Unlock()
   378→	registries = append(registries, r)
   379→
   380→	return r
   381→}
   382→
   383→// NewKubeRegistry creates a new vanilla Registry
   384→func NewKubeRegistry() KubeRegistry {
   385→	r := newKubeRegistry(BuildVersion())
   386→	return r
   387→}
   388→
   389→func (r *kubeRegistry) RegisterMetaMetrics() {
   390→	r.MustRegister(registeredMetricsTotal)
   391→	r.MustRegister(disabledMetricsTotal)
   392→	r.MustRegister(hiddenMetricsTotal)
   393→	r.MustRegister(cardinalityEnforcementUnexpectedCategorizationsTotal)
   394→}

Registerable is an interface for a collector metric which we\n// will register with KubeRegistry.\ntype Registerable interface {\n\tprometheus.Collector\n\n\t// Create will mark deprecated state for the collector\n\tCreate(version *semver.Version) bool\n\n\t// ClearState will clear all the states marked by Create.\n\tClearState()\n\n\t// FQName returns the fully-qualified metric name of the collector.\n\tFQName() string\n}\n\ntype resettable interface {\n\tReset()\n}\n\n// KubeRegistry is an interface which implements a subset of prometheus.Registerer and\n// prometheus.Gatherer interfaces\ntype KubeRegistry interface {\n\t// Deprecated\n\tRawMustRegister(...prometheus.Collector)\n\t// CustomRegister is our internal variant of Prometheus registry.Register\n\tCustomRegister(c StableCollector) error\n\t// CustomMustRegister is our internal variant of Prometheus registry.MustRegister\n\tCustomMustRegister(cs ...StableCollector)\n\t// Register conforms to Prometheus registry.Register\n\tRegister(Registerable) error\n\t// MustRegister conforms to Prometheus registry.MustRegister\n\tMustRegister(...Registerable)\n\t// Unregister conforms to Prometheus registry.Unregister\n\tUnregister(collector Collector) bool\n\t// Gather conforms to Prometheus gatherer.Gather\n\tGather() ([]*dto.MetricFamily, error)\n\t// Reset invokes the Reset() function on all items in the registry\n\t// which are added as resettables.\n\tReset()\n\t// RegisterMetaMetrics registers metrics about the number of registered metrics.\n\tRegisterMetaMetrics()\n\t// Registerer exposes the underlying prometheus registerer\n\tRegisterer() prometheus.Registerer\n\t// Gatherer exposes the underlying prometheus gatherer\n\tGatherer() prometheus.Gatherer\n}\n\n// kubeRegistry is a wrapper around a prometheus registry-type object. Upon initialization\n// the kubernetes binary version information is loaded into the registry object, so that\n// automatic behavior can be configured for metric versioning.\ntype kubeRegistry struct {\n\tPromRegistry\n\tversion              semver.Version\n\thiddenCollectors     map[string]Registerable // stores all collectors that has been hidden\n\tstableCollectors     []StableCollector       // stores all stable collector\n\thiddenCollectorsLock sync.RWMutex\n\tstableCollectorsLock sync.RWMutex\n\tresetLock            sync.RWMutex\n\tresettables          []resettable\n}\n\n// Register registers a new Collector to be included in metrics\n// collection. It returns an error if the descriptors provided by the\n// Collector are invalid or if they — in combination with descriptors of\n// already registered Collectors — do not fulfill the consistency and\n// uniqueness criteria described in the documentation of metric.Desc.\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) Register(c Registerable) error {\n\tif c.Create(&kr.version) {\n\t\tdefer kr.addResettable(c)\n\t\treturn kr.PromRegistry.Register(c)\n\t}\n\n\tkr.trackHiddenCollector(c)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Registerer exposes the underlying prometheus.Registerer\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) Registerer() prometheus.Registerer {\n\treturn kr.PromRegistry\n}\n\n// Gatherer exposes the underlying prometheus.Gatherer\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) Gatherer() prometheus.Gatherer {\n\treturn kr.PromRegistry\n}\n\n// MustRegister works like Register but registers any number of\n// Collectors and panics upon the first registration that causes an\n// error.\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) MustRegister(cs ...Registerable) {\n\tmetrics := make([]prometheus.Collector, 0, len(cs))\n\tfor _, c := range cs {\n\t\tif c.Create(&kr.version) {\n\t\t\tmetrics = append(metrics, c)\n\t\t\tkr.addResettable(c)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tkr.trackHiddenCollector(c)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tkr.PromRegistry.MustRegister(metrics...)\n}\n\n// CustomRegister registers a new custom collector.\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) CustomRegister(c StableCollector) error {\n\tkr.trackStableCollectors(c)\n\tdefer kr.addResettable(c)\n\tif c.Create(&kr.version, c) {\n\t\treturn kr.PromRegistry.Register(c)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// CustomMustRegister works like CustomRegister but registers any number of\n// StableCollectors and panics upon the first registration that causes an\n// error.\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) CustomMustRegister(cs ...StableCollector) {\n\tkr.trackStableCollectors(cs...)\n\tcollectors := make([]prometheus.Collector, 0, len(cs))\n\tfor _, c := range cs {\n\t\tif c.Create(&kr.version, c) {\n\t\t\tkr.addResettable(c)\n\t\t\tcollectors = append(collectors, c)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tkr.PromRegistry.MustRegister(collectors...)\n}\n\n// RawMustRegister takes a native prometheus.Collector and registers the collector\n// to the registry. This bypasses metrics safety checks, so should only be used\n// to register custom prometheus collectors.\n//\n// Deprecated\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) RawMustRegister(cs ...prometheus.Collector) {\n\tkr.PromRegistry.MustRegister(cs...)\n\tfor _, c := range cs {\n\t\tkr.addResettable(c)\n\t}\n}\n\n// addResettable will automatically add our metric to our reset\n// list if it satisfies the interface\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) addResettable(i interface{}) {\n\tkr.resetLock.Lock()\n\tdefer kr.resetLock.Unlock()\n\tif resettable, ok := i.(resettable); ok {\n\t\tkr.resettables = append(kr.resettables, resettable)\n\t}\n}\n\n// Unregister unregisters the Collector that equals the Collector passed\n// in as an argument.  (Two Collectors are considered equal if their\n// Describe method yields the same set of descriptors.) The function\n// returns whether a Collector was unregistered. Note that an unchecked\n// Collector cannot be unregistered (as its Describe method does not\n// yield any descriptor).\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) Unregister(collector Collector) bool {\n\treturn kr.PromRegistry.Unregister(collector)\n}\n\n// Gather calls the Collect method of the registered Collectors and then\n// gathers the collected metrics into a lexicographically sorted slice\n// of uniquely named MetricFamily protobufs. Gather ensures that the\n// returned slice is valid and self-consistent so that it can be used\n// for valid exposition. As an exception to the strict consistency\n// requirements described for metric.Desc, Gather will tolerate\n// different sets of label names for metrics of the same metric family.\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) Gather() ([]*dto.MetricFamily, error) {\n\treturn kr.PromRegistry.Gather()\n}\n\n// trackHiddenCollector stores all hidden collectors.\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) trackHiddenCollector(c Registerable) {\n\tkr.hiddenCollectorsLock.Lock()\n\tdefer kr.hiddenCollectorsLock.Unlock()\n\n\tkr.hiddenCollectors[c.FQName()] = c\n\thiddenMetricsTotal.Inc()\n}\n\n// trackStableCollectors stores all custom collectors.\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) trackStableCollectors(cs ...StableCollector) {\n\tkr.stableCollectorsLock.Lock()\n\tdefer kr.stableCollectorsLock.Unlock()\n\n\tkr.stableCollectors = append(kr.stableCollectors, cs...)\n}\n\n// enableHiddenCollectors will re-register all of the hidden collectors.\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) enableHiddenCollectors() {\n\tif len(kr.hiddenCollectors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tkr.hiddenCollectorsLock.Lock()\n\tcs := make([]Registerable, 0, len(kr.hiddenCollectors))\n\n\tfor _, c := range kr.hiddenCollectors {\n\t\tc.ClearState()\n\t\tcs = append(cs, c)\n\t}\n\n\tkr.hiddenCollectors = make(map[string]Registerable)\n\tkr.hiddenCollectorsLock.Unlock()\n\tkr.MustRegister(cs...)\n}\n\n// enableHiddenStableCollectors will re-register the stable collectors if there is one or more hidden metrics in it.\n// Since we can not register a metrics twice, so we have to unregister first then register again.\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) enableHiddenStableCollectors() {\n\tif len(kr.stableCollectors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tkr.stableCollectorsLock.Lock()\n\n\tcs := make([]StableCollector, 0, len(kr.stableCollectors))\n\tfor _, c := range kr.stableCollectors {\n\t\tif len(c.HiddenMetrics()) > 0 {\n\t\t\tkr.Unregister(c) // unregister must happens before clear state, otherwise no metrics would be unregister\n\t\t\tc.ClearState()\n\t\t\tcs = append(cs, c)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tkr.stableCollectors = nil\n\tkr.stableCollectorsLock.Unlock()\n\tkr.CustomMustRegister(cs...)\n}\n\n// Reset invokes Reset on all metrics that are resettable.\nfunc (kr *kubeRegistry) Reset() {\n\tkr.resetLock.RLock()\n\tdefer kr.resetLock.RUnlock()\n\tfor _, r := range kr.resettables {\n\t\tr.Reset()\n\t}\n}\n\n// BuildVersion is a helper function that can be easily mocked.\nvar BuildVersion = version.Get\n\nfunc newKubeRegistry(v apimachineryversion.Info) *kubeRegistry {\n\tr := &kubeRegistry{\n\t\tPromRegistry:     prometheus.NewRegistry(),\n\t\tversion:          parseVersion(v),\n\t\thiddenCollectors: make(map[string]Registerable),\n\t\tresettables:      make([]resettable, 0),\n\t}\n\n\tregistriesLock.Lock()\n\tdefer registriesLock.Unlock()\n\tregistries = append(registries, r)\n\n\treturn r\n}\n\n// NewKubeRegistry creates a new vanilla Registry\nfunc NewKubeRegistry() KubeRegistry {\n\tr := newKubeRegistry(BuildVersion())\n\treturn r\n}\n\nfunc (r *kubeRegistry) RegisterMetaMetrics() {\n\tr.MustRegister(registeredMetricsTotal)\n\tr.MustRegister(disabledMetricsTotal)\n\tr.MustRegister(hiddenMetricsTotal)\n\tr.MustRegister(cardinalityEnforcementUnexpectedCategorizationsTotal)\n}\n",     17→package metrics
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"io"
    21→	"net/http"
    22→	"time"
    23→
    24→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promhttp"
    25→)
    26→
    27→var (
    28→	processStartedAt time.Time
    29→)
    30→
    31→func init() {
    32→	processStartedAt = time.Now()
    33→}
    34→
    35→// These constants cause handlers serving metrics to behave as described if
    36→// errors are encountered.
    37→const (
    38→	// HTTPErrorOnError serve an HTTP status code 500 upon the first error
    39→	// encountered. Report the error message in the body.
    40→	HTTPErrorOnError promhttp.HandlerErrorHandling = iota
    41→
    42→	// ContinueOnError ignore errors and try to serve as many metrics as possible.
    43→	// However, if no metrics can be served, serve an HTTP status code 500 and the
    44→	// last error message in the body. Only use this in deliberate "best
    45→	// effort" metrics collection scenarios. In this case, it is highly
    46→	// recommended to provide other means of detecting errors: By setting an
    47→	// ErrorLog in HandlerOpts, the errors are logged. By providing a
    48→	// Registry in HandlerOpts, the exposed metrics include an error counter
    49→	// "promhttp_metric_handler_errors_total", which can be used for
    50→	// alerts.
    51→	ContinueOnError
    52→
    53→	// PanicOnError panics upon the first error encountered (useful for "crash only" apps).
    54→	PanicOnError
    55→)
    56→
    57→// HandlerOpts specifies options how to serve metrics via an http.Handler. The
    58→// zero value of HandlerOpts is a reasonable default.
    59→type HandlerOpts promhttp.HandlerOpts
    60→
    61→func (ho *HandlerOpts) toPromhttpHandlerOpts() promhttp.HandlerOpts {
    62→	ho.ProcessStartTime = processStartedAt
    63→	return promhttp.HandlerOpts(*ho)
    64→}
    65→
    66→// HandlerFor returns an uninstrumented http.Handler for the provided
    67→// Gatherer. The behavior of the Handler is defined by the provided
    68→// HandlerOpts. Thus, HandlerFor is useful to create http.Handlers for custom
    69→// Gatherers, with non-default HandlerOpts, and/or with custom (or no)
    70→// instrumentation. Use the InstrumentMetricHandler function to apply the same
    71→// kind of instrumentation as it is used by the Handler function.
    72→func HandlerFor(reg Gatherer, opts HandlerOpts) http.Handler {
    73→	return promhttp.HandlerFor(reg, opts.toPromhttpHandlerOpts())
    74→}
    75→
    76→// HandlerWithReset return an http.Handler with Reset
    77→func HandlerWithReset(reg KubeRegistry, opts HandlerOpts) http.Handler {
    78→	defaultHandler := promhttp.HandlerFor(reg, opts.toPromhttpHandlerOpts())
    79→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    80→		if r.Method == http.MethodDelete {
    81→			reg.Reset()
    82→			io.WriteString(w, "metrics reset\n")
    83→			return
    84→		}
    85→		defaultHandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)
    86→	})
    87→}

Only use this in deliberate \"best\n\t// effort\" metrics collection scenarios. In this case, it is highly\n\t// recommended to provide other means of detecting errors: By setting an\n\t// ErrorLog in HandlerOpts, the errors are logged. The\n// zero value of HandlerOpts is a reasonable default.\ntype HandlerOpts promhttp.HandlerOpts\n\nfunc (ho *HandlerOpts) toPromhttpHandlerOpts() promhttp.HandlerOpts {\n\tho.ProcessStartTime = processStartedAt\n\treturn promhttp.HandlerOpts(*ho)\n}\n\n// HandlerFor returns an uninstrumented http.Handler for the provided\n// Gatherer. Thus, HandlerFor is useful to create http.Handlers for custom\n// Gatherers, with non-default HandlerOpts, and/or with custom (or no)\n// instrumentation. 1→package server
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"net"
     6→	"net/http"
     7→	"time"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/grafana/dskit/services"
    10→	"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/infra/log"
    11→	"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/setting"
    12→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promhttp"
    13→)
    14→
    15→type instrumentationService struct {
    16→	*services.BasicService
    17→	cfg      *setting.Cfg
    18→	httpServ *http.Server
    19→	log      log.Logger
    20→	errChan  chan error
    21→}
    22→
    23→func NewInstrumentationService(log log.Logger, cfg *setting.Cfg) (*instrumentationService, error) {
    24→	s := &instrumentationService{log: log, cfg: cfg}
    25→	s.BasicService = services.NewBasicService(s.start, s.running, s.stop)
    26→	return s, nil
    27→}
    28→
    29→func (s *instrumentationService) start(ctx context.Context) error {
    30→	s.httpServ = s.newInstrumentationServer(ctx)
    31→	s.errChan = make(chan error)
    32→	go func() {
    33→		s.errChan <- s.httpServ.ListenAndServe()
    34→	}()
    35→	return nil
    36→}
    37→
    38→func (s *instrumentationService) running(ctx context.Context) error {
    39→	select {
    40→	case <-ctx.Done():
    41→		return nil
    42→	case err := <-s.errChan:
    43→		return err
    44→	}
    45→}
    46→
    47→func (s *instrumentationService) stop(failureReason error) error {
    48→	s.log.Info("stopping instrumentation server", "reason", failureReason)
    49→	if err := s.httpServ.Shutdown(context.Background()); err != nil {
    50→		s.log.Error("failed to shutdown instrumentation server", "error", err)
    51→		return err
    52→	}
    53→
    54→	return nil
    55→}
    56→
    57→func (s *instrumentationService) newInstrumentationServer(ctx context.Context) *http.Server {
    58→	router := http.NewServeMux()
    59→	router.Handle("/metrics", promhttp.Handler())
    60→
    61→	srv := &http.Server{
    62→		// 5s timeout for header reads to avoid Slowloris attacks (https://thetooth.io/blog/slowloris-attack/)
    63→		ReadHeaderTimeout: 5 * time.Second,
    64→		Addr:              ":" + s.cfg.HTTPPort,
    65→		Handler:           router,
    66→		BaseContext:       func(_ net.Listener) context.Context { return ctx },
    67→	}
    68→
    69→	return srv
    70→}
    71→

[metadata] {"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "/workspace/grafana--v11.4.0/pkg/server/instrumentation_service.go", "content": "package server\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"net\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/grafana/dskit/services\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/infra/log\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/setting\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promhttp\"\n)\n\ntype instrumentationService struct {\n\t*services.BasicService\n\tcfg      *setting.Cfg\n\thttpServ *http.Server\n\tlog      log.Logger\n\terrChan  chan error\n}\n\nfunc NewInstrumentationService(log log.Logger, cfg *setting.Cfg) (*instrumentationService, error) {\n\ts := &instrumentationService{log: log, cfg: cfg}\n\ts.BasicService = services.NewBasicService(s.start, s.running, s.stop)\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *instrumentationService) start(ctx context.Context) error {\n\ts.httpServ = s.newInstrumentationServer(ctx)\n\ts.errChan = make(chan error)\n\tgo func() {\n\t\ts.errChan <- s.httpServ.ListenAndServe()\n\t}()\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *instrumentationService) running(ctx context.Context) error {\n\tselect {\n\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\treturn nil\n\tcase err := <-s.errChan:\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (s *instrumentationService) stop(failureReason error) error {\n\ts.log.Info(\"stopping instrumentation server\", \"reason\", failureReason)\n\tif err := s.httpServ.Shutdown(context.Background()); err != nil {\n\t\ts.log.Error(\"failed to shutdown instrumentation server\", \"error\", err)\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *instrumentationService) newInstrumentationServer(ctx context.Context) *http.Server {\n\trouter := http.NewServeMux()\n\trouter.Handle(\"/metrics\", promhttp.Handler())\n\n\tsrv := &http.Server{\n\t\t// 5s timeout for header reads to avoid Slowloris attacks (https://thetooth.io/blog/slowloris-attack/)\n\t\tReadHeaderTimeout: 5 * time.Second,\n\t\tAddr:              \":\" + s.cfg.HTTPPort,\n\t\tHandler:           router,\n\t\tBaseContext:       func(_ net.Listener) context.Context { return ctx },\n\t}\n\n\treturn srv\n}\n", "numLines": 71, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 71}}

    17→package app
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"net/http"
    21→	goruntime "runtime"
    22→
    23→	genericapifilters "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters"
    24→	apirequest "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request"
    25→	apiserver "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server"
    26→	genericfilters "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters"
    27→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/mux"
    28→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes"
    29→	"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme"
    30→	componentbaseconfig "k8s.io/component-base/config"
    31→	"k8s.io/component-base/configz"
    32→	"k8s.io/component-base/logs"
    33→	"k8s.io/component-base/metrics/legacyregistry"
    34→	_ "k8s.io/component-base/metrics/prometheus/workqueue" // for workqueue metric registration
    35→)
    36→
    37→// BuildHandlerChain builds a handler chain with a base handler and CompletedConfig.
    38→func BuildHandlerChain(apiHandler http.Handler, authorizationInfo *apiserver.AuthorizationInfo, authenticationInfo *apiserver.AuthenticationInfo) http.Handler {
    39→	requestInfoResolver := &apirequest.RequestInfoFactory{}
    40→	failedHandler := genericapifilters.Unauthorized(scheme.Codecs)
    41→
    42→	handler := apiHandler
    43→	if authorizationInfo != nil {
    44→		handler = genericapifilters.WithAuthorization(apiHandler, authorizationInfo.Authorizer, scheme.Codecs)
    45→	}
    46→	if authenticationInfo != nil {
    47→		handler = genericapifilters.WithAuthentication(handler, authenticationInfo.Authenticator, failedHandler, nil, nil)
    48→	}
    49→	handler = genericapifilters.WithRequestInfo(handler, requestInfoResolver)
    50→	handler = genericapifilters.WithCacheControl(handler)
    51→	handler = genericfilters.WithHTTPLogging(handler)
    52→	handler = genericfilters.WithPanicRecovery(handler, requestInfoResolver)
    53→
    54→	return handler
    55→}
    56→
    57→// NewBaseHandler takes in CompletedConfig and returns a handler.
    58→func NewBaseHandler(c *componentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration, healthzHandler http.Handler) *mux.PathRecorderMux {
    59→	mux := mux.NewPathRecorderMux("controller-manager")
    60→	mux.Handle("/healthz", healthzHandler)
    61→	if c.EnableProfiling {
    62→		routes.Profiling{}.Install(mux)
    63→		if c.EnableContentionProfiling {
    64→			goruntime.SetBlockProfileRate(1)
    65→		}
    66→		routes.DebugFlags{}.Install(mux, "v", routes.StringFlagPutHandler(logs.GlogSetter))
    67→	}
    68→	configz.InstallHandler(mux)
    69→	mux.Handle("/metrics", legacyregistry.Handler())
    70→
    71→	return mux
    72→}
    73→

    29→
    30→// DefaultMetrics installs the default prometheus metrics handler
    31→type DefaultMetrics struct{}
    32→
    33→// Install adds the DefaultMetrics handler
    34→func (m DefaultMetrics) Install(c *mux.PathRecorderMux) {
    35→	register()
    36→	c.Handle("/metrics", legacyregistry.Handler())
    37→}
    38→
    39→// MetricsWithReset install the prometheus metrics handler extended with support for the DELETE method
    40→// which resets the metrics.
    41→type MetricsWithReset struct{}
    42→
    43→// Install adds the MetricsWithReset handler
    44→func (m MetricsWithReset) Install(c *mux.PathRecorderMux) {
    45→	register()
    46→	c.Handle("/metrics", legacyregistry.HandlerWithReset())
    47→}
    48→
    49→// register apiserver and etcd metrics
    50→func register() {
    51→	apimetrics.Register()
    52→	cachermetrics.Register()
    53→	etcd3metrics.Register()
    54→	flowcontrolmetrics.Register()
    55→	peerproxymetrics.Register()
    56→	handlersmetrics.Register()
    57→}

    30→	httpRequestsInFlight := prometheus.NewGauge(
    31→		prometheus.GaugeOpts{

[metadata] {"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "/workspace/grafana--v11.4.0/pkg/middleware/request_metrics.go", "content": "package middleware\n\nimport (\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/infra/log\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/infra/metrics\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/infra/tracing\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/middleware/requestmeta\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/services/featuremgmt\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/setting\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/web\"\n)\n\nvar (\n\t// DefBuckets are histogram buckets for the response time (in seconds)\n\t// of a network service, including one that is responding very slowly.\n\tdefBuckets = []float64{.005, .01, .025, .05, .1, .25, .5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10, 25}\n)\n\n// RequestMetrics is a middleware handler that instruments the request.\nfunc RequestMetrics(features featuremgmt.FeatureToggles, cfg *setting.Cfg, promRegister prometheus.Registerer) web.Middleware {\n\tlog := log.New(\"middleware.request-metrics\")\n\n\thttpRequestsInFlight := prometheus.NewGauge(\n\t\tprometheus.GaugeOpts{\n\t\t\tNamespace: \"grafana\",\n\t\t\tName:      \"http_request_in_flight\",\n\t\t\tHelp:      \"A gauge of requests currently being served by Grafana.\",\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\n\thistogramLabels := []string{\"handler\", \"status_code\", \"method\", \"status_source\", \"slo_group\"}\n\n\tif cfg.MetricsIncludeTeamLabel {\n\t\thistogramLabels = append(histogramLabels, \"grafana_team\")\n\t}\n\n\thistogramOptions := prometheus.HistogramOpts{\n\t\tNamespace: \"grafana\",\n\t\tName:      \"http_request_duration_seconds\",\n\t\tHelp:      \"Histogram of latencies for HTTP requests.\",\n\t\tBuckets:   defBuckets,\n\t}\n", "numLines": 50, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 190}}

1→package metrics
     2→
     3→import "github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"
     4→
     5→// PostPostFrontendMetricsCommand sent by frontend to record frontend metrics
     6→type PostFrontendMetricsCommand struct {
     7→	Events []FrontendMetricEvent `json:"events"`
     8→}
     9→
    10→// FrontendMetricEvent a single metric measurement event
    11→type FrontendMetricEvent struct {
    12→	Name  string  `json:"name"`
    13→	Value float64 `json:"value"`
    14→}
    15→
    16→// FrontendMetricsRecorder handles the recording of the event, ie passes it to a prometheus metric
    17→type FrontendMetricsRecorder func(event FrontendMetricEvent)
    18→
    19→// FrontendMetrics contains all the valid frontend metrics and a handler function for recording events
    20→var FrontendMetrics map[string]FrontendMetricsRecorder = map[string]FrontendMetricsRecorder{}
    21→
    22→func registerFrontendHistogram(reg prometheus.Registerer, name string, help string) {
    23→	defBuckets := []float64{.1, .25, .5, 1, 1.5, 2, 5, 10, 20, 40}
    24→
    25→	histogram := prometheus.NewHistogram(prometheus.HistogramOpts{
    26→		Name:      name,
    27→		Help:      help,
    28→		Buckets:   defBuckets,
    29→		Namespace: ExporterName,
    30→	})
    31→
    32→	FrontendMetrics[name] = func(event FrontendMetricEvent) {
    33→		histogram.Observe(event.Value)
    34→	}
    35→
    36→	reg.MustRegister(histogram)
    37→}
    38→
    39→func initFrontendMetrics(r prometheus.Registerer) {
    40→	registerFrontendHistogram(r, "frontend_boot_load_time_seconds", "Frontend boot time measurement")
    41→	registerFrontendHistogram(r, "frontend_boot_first_paint_time_seconds", "Frontend boot first paint")
    42→	registerFrontendHistogram(r, "frontend_boot_first_contentful_paint_time_seconds", "Frontend boot first contentful paint")
    43→	registerFrontendHistogram(r, "frontend_boot_js_done_time_seconds", "Frontend boot initial js load")
    44→	registerFrontendHistogram(r, "frontend_boot_css_time_seconds", "Frontend boot initial css load")
    45→	registerFrontendHistogram(r, "frontend_plugins_preload_ms", "Frontend preload plugin time measurement")
    46→}

40}\n\n\thistogram := prometheus.NewHistogram(prometheus.HistogramOpts{\n\t\tName:      name,\n\t\tHelp:      help,\n\t\tBuckets:   defBuckets,\n\t\tNamespace: ExporterName,\n\t})\n\n\tFrontendMetrics[name] = func(event FrontendMetricEvent) {\n\t\thistogram.Observe(event.Value)\n\t}\n\n\treg.MustRegister(histogram)\n}\n\nfunc initFrontendMetrics(r prometheus.Registerer) {\n\tregisterFrontendHistogram(r, \"frontend_boot_load_time_seconds\", \"Frontend boot time measurement\")\n\tregisterFrontendHistogram(r, \"frontend_boot_first_paint_time_seconds\", \"Frontend boot first paint\")\n\tregisterFrontendHistogram(r, \"frontend_boot_first_contentful_paint_time_seconds\", \"Frontend boot first contentful paint\")\n\tregisterFrontendHistogram(r, \"frontend_boot_js_done_time_seconds\", \"Frontend boot initial js load\")\n\tregisterFrontendHistogram(r, \"frontend_boot_css_time_seconds\", \"Frontend boot initial css load\")\n\tregisterFrontendHistogram(r, \"frontend_plugins_preload_ms\", \"Frontend preload plugin time measurement\")\n}\n", "numLines": 47, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 47}}

1→package metrics
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"strings"
     6→	"time"
     7→
     8→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/infra/metrics/graphitebridge"
    11→)
    12→
    13→func (im *InternalMetricsService) readSettings() error {
    14→	var section, err = im.Cfg.Raw.GetSection("metrics")
    15→	if err != nil {
    16→		return fmt.Errorf("unable to find metrics config section: %w", err)
    17→	}
    18→
    19→	im.intervalSeconds = section.Key("interval_seconds").MustInt64(10)
    20→
    21→	if err := im.parseGraphiteSettings(); err != nil {
    22→		return fmt.Errorf("unable to parse metrics graphite section: %w", err)
    23→	}
    24→
    25→	return nil
    26→}
    27→
    28→func (im *InternalMetricsService) parseGraphiteSettings() error {
    29→	graphiteSection, err := im.Cfg.Raw.GetSection("metrics.graphite")
    30→	if err != nil {
    31→		return nil
    32→	}
    33→
    34→	address := graphiteSection.Key("address").String()
    35→	if address == "" {
    36→		return nil
    37→	}
    38→
    39→	bridgeCfg := &graphitebridge.Config{
    40→		URL:             address,
    41→		Prefix:          graphiteSection.Key("prefix").MustString("prod.grafana.%(instance_name)s"),
    42→		CountersAsDelta: true,
    43→		Gatherer:        prometheus.DefaultGatherer,
    44→		Interval:        time.Duration(im.intervalSeconds) * time.Second,
    45→		Timeout:         10 * time.Second,
    46→		Logger:          &logWrapper{logger: metricsLogger},
    47→		ErrorHandling:   graphitebridge.ContinueOnError,
    48→	}
    49→
    50→	safeInstanceName := strings.ReplaceAll(im.Cfg.InstanceName, ".", "_")
    51→	prefix := graphiteSection.Key("prefix").Value()
    52→
    53→	if prefix == "" {
    54→		prefix = "prod.grafana.%(instance_name)s."
    55→	}
    56→
    57→	bridgeCfg.Prefix = strings.ReplaceAll(prefix, "%(instance_name)s", safeInstanceName)
    58→
    59→	im.graphiteCfg = bridgeCfg
    60→	return nil
    61→}
    62→

[metadata] {"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "/workspace/grafana--v11.4.0/pkg/infra/metrics/settings.go", "content": "package metrics\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/infra/metrics/graphitebridge\"\n)\n\nfunc (im *InternalMetricsService) readSettings() error {\n\tvar section, err = im.Cfg.Raw.GetSection(\"metrics\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to find metrics config section: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tim.intervalSeconds = section.Key(\"interval_seconds\").MustInt64(10)\n\n\tif err := im.parseGraphiteSettings(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to parse metrics graphite section: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (im *InternalMetricsService) parseGraphiteSettings() error {\n\tgraphiteSection, err := im.Cfg.Raw.GetSection(\"metrics.graphite\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\taddress := graphiteSection.Key(\"address\").String()\n\tif address == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tbridgeCfg := &graphitebridge.Config{\n\t\tURL:             address,\n\t\tPrefix:          graphiteSection.Key(\"prefix\").MustString(\"prod.grafana.%(instance_name)s\"),\n\t\tCountersAsDelta: true,\n\t\tGatherer:        prometheus.DefaultGatherer,\n\t\tInterval:        time.Duration(im.intervalSeconds) * time.Second,\n\t\tTimeout:         10 * time.Second,\n\t\tLogger:          &logWrapper{logger: metricsLogger},\n\t\tErrorHandling:   graphitebridge.ContinueOnError,\n\t}\n\n\tsafeInstanceName := strings.ReplaceAll(im.Cfg.InstanceName, \".\", \"_\")\n\tprefix := graphiteSection.Key(\"prefix\").Value()\n\n\tif prefix == \"\" {\n\t\tprefix = \"prod.grafana.%(instance_name)s.\"\n\t}\n\n\tbridgeCfg.Prefix = strings.ReplaceAll(prefix, \"%(instance_name)s\", safeInstanceName)\n\n\tim.graphiteCfg = bridgeCfg\n\treturn nil\n}\n", "numLines": 62, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 62}}

  "text": "Both Kubernetes (v1.32.0) and Grafana (v11.4.0) use the Prometheus client_golang library to implement metrics exposition, but they organize their metrics infrastructure quite differently.\n\n## Kubernetes (kubernetes/kubernetes)\n\n### Core Metrics Infrastructure: k8s.io/component-base/metrics\n\nKubernetes builds a **stability-framework wrapper** around the raw Prometheus library, located in `staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/`.\n\n**Registry layer** (`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/registry.go`):\n- `KubeRegistry` interface wraps `prometheus.Registerer`/`prometheus.Gatherer` with Kubernetes-specific lifecycle methods (`Register`, `MustRegister`, `CustomRegister`, `CustomMustRegister`, `RawMustRegister`, `Gather`, `Reset`, `RegisterMetaMetrics`).\n- `kubeRegistry` struct implements `KubeRegistry`, holding a `PromRegistry` (a `prometheus.Registry`), the binary version (semver), and maps of `hiddenCollectors` and `stableCollectors`.\n- `NewKubeRegistry()` / `newKubeRegistry()` construct a new instance, appending it to a global `registries` slice so that `SetShowHidden()` can re-register hidden metrics at runtime.\n- The `Registerable` interface requires `prometheus.Collector` + `Create(version)`, `ClearState()`, and `FQName()` — enabling version-gated metric hiding/deprecation.\n- `SetShowHidden()`, `SetDisabledMetric()`, `ValidateShowHiddenMetricsVersion()` implement Kubernetes's metric deprecation lifecycle.\n- Meta-metrics `registeredMetricsTotal`, `disabledMetricsTotal`, `hiddenMetricsTotal` are registered via `RegisterMetaMetrics()`.\n\n**Global/legacy registry** (`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/legacyregistry/registry.go`):\n- `defaultRegistry` is the singleton `KubeRegistry` used by nearly all Kubernetes components.\n- Package-level `MustRegister`, `Register`, `RawMustRegister`, `CustomRegister`, `CustomMustRegister`, `Registerer`, `DefaultGatherer` functions delegate to `defaultRegistry`.\n- In `init()`, process and Go runtime collectors (`collectors.NewProcessCollector`, `collectors.NewGoCollector`) are registered, and meta-metrics are initialized.\n- `Handler()` returns an HTTP handler via `promhttp.InstrumentMetricHandler(prometheus.DefaultRegisterer, promhttp.HandlerFor(defaultRegistry, ...))` — this is the core exposition handler.\n- `HandlerWithReset()` additionally supports DELETE to reset all metric values.\n\n**HTTP exposition** (`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/http.go`):\n- `HandlerFor(reg Gatherer, opts HandlerOpts)` wraps `promhttp.HandlerFor` with Kubernetes-specific opts (process start time).\n- `HandlerWithReset(reg KubeRegistry, opts HandlerOpts)` wraps the handler to call `reg.Reset()` on DELETE requests.\n\n**Collector abstraction** (`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/collector.go`):\n- `StableCollector` interface extends `prometheus.Collector` with `DescribeWithStability`, `CollectWithStability`, `Create(version, self)`, `ClearState()`, `HiddenMetrics()`.\n- `BaseStableCollector` provides a base implementation supporting hidden/registerable descriptor tracking per binary version.\n\n**Metric type wrappers**: `counter.go` (`Counter`), `gauge.go` (`Gauge`), `histogram.go` (`Histogram`), `summary.go` (`Summary`) all wrap their Prometheus counterparts with a `lazyMetric` pattern (lazy initialization gated on `Create(version)`).\n\n**Prometheus exposition endpoint installation**:\n- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes/metrics.go`: `DefaultMetrics` and `MetricsWithReset` types install the `/metrics` endpoint on a `mux.PathRecorderMux` via `c.Handle(\"/metrics\", legacyregistry.Handler())`. The `register()` function triggers registration of apiserver-specific metric collectors (`apimetrics.Register()`, `cachermetrics.Register()`, `etcd3metrics.Register()`, `flowcontrolmetrics.Register()`, `peerproxymetrics.Register()`, `handlersmetrics.Register()`).\n- `staging/src/k8s.io/controller-manager/app/serve.go`: `NewBaseHandler()` adds `mux.Handle(\"/metrics\", legacyregistry.Handler())` for controller-manager components.\n- `cmd/kube-proxy/app/server.go` and `cmd/kube-scheduler/app/server.go` similarly call `legacyregistry.Handler()` to install the `/metrics` endpoint.\n- `pkg/kubelet/server/server.go` wires the kubelet's `/metrics` endpoint.\n\n**Sub-packages for domain-specific metrics collectors** (`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/metrics/prometheus/`):\n- `workqueue/metrics.go` — registers workqueue metrics\n- `feature/metrics.go` — feature gate metrics\n- `slis/metrics.go`, `slis/registry.go`, `slis/routes.go` — SLI/SLO metrics with dedicated HTTP routes\n- `restclient/metrics.go` — REST client metrics\n- `clientgo/metrics.go` — client-go metrics aggregator (imports leaderelection, restclient, workqueue)\n- `controllers/metrics.go`, `meta/metrics.go`, `version/metrics.go` — controller and version metrics\n\n---\n\n## Grafana (grafana/grafana)\n\nGrafana uses the Prometheus client_golang library more directly, with its own registry provisioning and a dual-gatherer model that combines Kubernetes-style (via `k8s.io/component-base/metrics/legacyregistry`) and native Prometheus registries.\n\n**Core metrics package** (`pkg/infra/metrics/`):\n\n- `metrics.go`: Declares `ExporterName = \"grafana\"` as the Prometheus namespace prefix and defines all global metric variables as bare `prometheus.*` types (e.g., `MInstanceStart prometheus.Counter`, `MPageStatus *prometheus.CounterVec`, `MStatTotalDashboards prometheus.Gauge`, etc.).\n  - `init()` sets up metric objects with the `grafana` namespace prefix.\n  - `initMetricVars(reg prometheus.Registerer)` performs bulk registration of all core Grafana metrics via `reg.MustRegister(...)`.\n\n- `service.go`: `ProvideService(cfg, reg prometheus.Registerer)` is the wire injection point that calls `initMetricVars(reg)` and `initFrontendMetrics(reg)` to register all metrics.\n  - `InternalMetricsService` is a background service that also bridges metrics to Graphite via `graphitebridge.NewBridge`.\n  - `ProvideRegisterer()` returns `legacyregistry.Registerer()` (from Kubernetes's component-base) as the global registerer — meaning Grafana reuses the Kubernetes global registry for metric registration.\n  - `ProvideGatherer()` constructs a **composite gatherer**: `newMultiRegistry(newAddPrefixWrapper(legacyregistry.DefaultGatherer), prometheus.DefaultGatherer)`. This combines metrics from the Kubernetes-style registry (with a `grafana_` prefix enforcement) and the default Prometheus registry.\n  - `addPrefixWrapper` ensures all metric names are prefixed with `grafana_` unless they already start with `grafana_` or `go_`.\n  - `multiRegistry` deduplicates metric names across the two gatherers (allowing `go_*` duplicates).\n\n- `frontendmetrics.go`: `initFrontendMetrics(r prometheus.Registerer)` registers frontend-specific histograms (boot time, first paint, etc.) under the `grafana` namespace.\n\n- `settings.go`: `readSettings()` configures the Graphite bridge interval and address; `parseGraphiteSettings()` populates a `graphitebridge.Config` using `prometheus.DefaultGatherer` for the bridge source.\n\n- `metricutil/utils.go`: Helper functions `NewCounterStartingAtZero` and `NewCounterVecStartingAtZero` initialize counters with a zero observation to guarantee metric time-series existence from startup.\n\n**HTTP /metrics endpoint exposition**:\n- `pkg/server/instrumentation_service.go`: `instrumentationService` / `NewInstrumentationService` creates a standalone HTTP server with `newInstrumentationServer()`, which registers `promhttp.Handler()` (using Prometheus's default gatherer) on `/metrics`. This provides an instrumentation-only endpoint on a separate port.\n- `pkg/api/http_server.go`: The main Grafana HTTP server (`HTTPServer`) also serves `/metrics` by calling `promhttp.HandlerFor(hs.promGatherer, promhttp.HandlerOpts{EnableOpenMetrics: true})` — using the composite `promGatherer` (which combines the Kubernetes legacy registry with the Prometheus default registry). This endpoint supports OpenMetrics format and optional basic authentication.\n\n**Request-level metrics middleware** (`pkg/middleware/request_metrics.go`): `RequestMetrics` is a web middleware that instruments HTTP requests with `http_request_in_flight` gauge and `http_request_duration_seconds` histogram, registered directly against the injected `prometheus.Registerer`.\n\n**Subsystem-specific metric collectors** (each subsystem creates its own scoped registry/sub-namespace):\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/metrics/` — alerting subsystem (scheduler, alertmanager, API, historian, state, multi-org, remote alertmanager, remote writer)\n- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/metrics.go`, `pkg/services/authn/authnimpl/metrics.go`, `pkg/services/auth/idimpl/metrics.go` — auth subsystem metrics\n- `pkg/services/dashboards/service/metrics.go`, `pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/metrics.go` — storage metrics\n- `pkg/infra/httpclient/httpclientprovider/prometheus_metrics_middleware.go` — HTTP client metrics\n\n---\n\n## Comparison\n\n| Aspect | Kubernetes | Grafana |\n|---|---|---|  \n| **Registry** | Custom `KubeRegistry` wrapping `prometheus.Registry`; global singleton in `legacyregistry.defaultRegistry` | Injects `prometheus.Registerer` (backed by k8s `legacyregistry.Registerer()`); composite gatherer combining two registries |\n| **Collector abstraction** | `Registerable` + `StableCollector` / `BaseStableCollector` for version-gated metric hiding | Direct `prometheus.Collector` types; no versioned lifecycle |\n| **Metric types** | Custom wrappers (`Counter`, `Gauge`, `Histogram`, `Summary`) with lazy initialization and stability levels | Native `prometheus.*` types directly |\n| **/metrics endpoint** | `legacyregistry.Handler()` → `promhttp.HandlerFor(defaultRegistry, ...)` installed via `DefaultMetrics.Install()` or `mux.Handle()` in each component | `promhttp.HandlerFor(compositeGatherer, ...)` in `HTTPServer`; separate `instrumentationService` with `promhttp.Handler()` |\n| **OpenMetrics** | Not explicitly enabled | Enabled via `HandlerOpts{EnableOpenMetrics: true}` |\n| **Extra exposition** | DELETE method on `/metrics` resets counters (`HandlerWithReset`) | Optional basic auth on `/metrics` endpoint |\n| **Metric naming** | Namespaced by subsystem (no global prefix enforcement) | `grafana_` namespace prefix enforced by `addPrefixWrapper` on the k8s legacy gatherer |\n| **Side-channel export** | Not built-in | Graphite bridge (`graphitebridge`) for pushing metrics |\n| **Subsystem organization** | Many per-package `metrics.go` files, each calling `legacyregistry.MustRegister` in a `Register()` function or `init()` | Per-subsystem `metrics.go` files that take `prometheus.Registerer` as a constructor argument |"
}
